raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":1.0,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.3,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.1":"Any opinion on this paper requires reconstructing its thesis (lexical ambiguity in LLM-based MT), the stated gap (zero-shot translation lacks an intent-surfacing mechanism), and the contribution claim (inference-time two-stage clarification preamble); these are the foundation for any meaningful assessment.","R1.2":"Evaluating whether the two-stage prompting logic is internally sound, whether the BabelNet-filtered benchmark construction properly operationalizes the problem, and whether the ablation conclusions (paraphrase > sense-glosses > topic) follow from the data all require logical-consistency scrutiny central to a paper opinion.","R1.3":"The paper asserts specific quantitative outcomes (COMET-22 +1.8 zh-en, +1.4 en-de; 62% human preference on 200 segments; within 0.3 COMET of NLLB-200-3.3B) that must be checked for alignment with the described experimental setup, baseline choices, and evaluation protocol.","R1.4":"The paper explicitly advances a strong first-to-do-X novelty claim ('to our knowledge, ClariNMT is the first translation framework to use self-generated multi-aspect clarification context...and the first to demonstrate...'); adjudicating this claim against prior disambiguation-augmented MT and prompting literature is load-bearing for any opinion on a research contribution."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Is your primary interest specifically in whether the novelty claim ('to our knowledge, the first...') holds up against prior work (yes), or do you want methodological soundness and evidentiary support examined with equal weight alongside novelty (no)?"}

rationale.R1.1: Any opinion on this paper requires reconstructing its thesis (lexical ambiguity in LLM-based MT), the stated gap (zero-shot translation lacks an intent-surfacing mechanism), and the contribution claim (inference-time two-stage clarification preamble); these are the foundation for any meaningful assessment.
rationale.R1.2: Evaluating whether the two-stage prompting logic is internally sound, whether the BabelNet-filtered benchmark construction properly operationalizes the problem, and whether the ablation conclusions (paraphrase > sense-glosses > topic) follow from the data all require logical-consistency scrutiny central to a paper opinion.
rationale.R1.3: The paper asserts specific quantitative outcomes (COMET-22 +1.8 zh-en, +1.4 en-de; 62% human preference on 200 segments; within 0.3 COMET of NLLB-200-3.3B) that must be checked for alignment with the described experimental setup, baseline choices, and evaluation protocol.
rationale.R1.4: The paper explicitly advances a strong first-to-do-X novelty claim ('to our knowledge, ClariNMT is the first translation framework to use self-generated multi-aspect clarification context...and the first to demonstrate...'); adjudicating this claim against prior disambiguation-augmented MT and prompting literature is load-bearing for any opinion on a research contribution.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary interest specifically in whether the novelty claim ('to our knowledge, the first...') holds up against prior work (yes), or do you want methodological soundness and evidentiary support examined with equal weight alongside novelty (no)?
